cover.headline: Built for factories that outgrew their registers.
cover.subline: ArchFlow builds custom operations software for Indian manufacturers — one system carrying orders, production, dispatch and accounts instead of scattered registers.
problemParas: A small factory runs fine on paper and spreadsheets. Orders in one file, dispatch in another, payments in a third. One person holds the links in his head. That works for years.
Then it grows. Ten people update the same numbers, never at the same time. Nobody can say where an order stands without three phone calls and a search through WhatsApp.

problemPains.title: One order, three versions
problemPains.body: Sales quotes one size, the job card carries another, the site measurement corrects both. The challan goes out on whichever sheet was open that morning.

problemPains.title: Status takes three phone calls
problemPains.body: Production asks accounts, accounts asks dispatch, dispatch asks the supervisor. By evening the answer arrives on WhatsApp, and nobody writes it back into the register.

problemPains.title: Month-end rebuilt by hand
problemPains.body: Dispatch registers, gate passes and payment entries are matched by hand for days. The numbers finally agree, and by then the month has already moved on.

build.title: What we build
build.lead: Six platform types. Each built around how your business already works, not configured from a template. They run together as one system, or any one can stand alone.

platforms.name: Quotation & MIS
platforms.body: Catalogue-aware pricing with your BOM rules. Versioned quotes on your letterhead, discount approvals by role, and one-click conversion into a confirmed order.

platforms.name: End-to-End Workflow Systems
platforms.body: Enquiry to installation on one platform. Every department works off the same record. Handoffs happen as status changes, not chased on WhatsApp.

platforms.name: Custom Sales CRM
platforms.body: Built around your real cycle: site visit, measurement, quote, revision, order. Territory and BDM hierarchies, site visits logged, follow-up escalation, lost-reason analytics.

platforms.name: Production & Dispatch
platforms.body: Job cards raised from confirmed orders. Shop-floor status updated by the operator, stores linked, vehicles scheduled, and site acknowledgement with snags captured.

platforms.name: Multi-location Operations
platforms.body: Company, region, branch, site — a real hierarchy. Each office sees its own data, leadership sees everything, and inter-branch transfers stay on record.

platforms.name: Leadership MIS Dashboards
platforms.body: Order book, dispatch status, collection due, region-wise pipeline — live, on your phone. Every tile drills down to the transaction behind the number.

build.flowTitle: One order, from enquiry to service
build.flowLead: Pranav Doors & Windows runs fourteen stages daily. These seven matter in every business. One record travels the whole way, picking up detail at each desk.

stages.name: Enquiry
stages.body: Lead captured, BDM assigned, site visit logged. Nothing sits unclaimed in someone's inbox.

stages.name: Quotation
stages.body: Measurements become a BOM-priced quote. Revisions versioned, discounts approved by role, sent same-day.

stages.name: Order
stages.body: PO confirmed against the winning version. The customer's full history on one screen.

stages.name: Production
stages.body: Job cards reach the shop floor. Operators update status; the office watches live.

stages.name: Dispatch
stages.body: Vehicle and driver assigned, challan raised, gate pass printed, site team notified automatically.

stages.name: Installation
stages.body: Site checklists on the installer's phone. Snags photographed, customer sign-off captured on site.

stages.name: Service
stages.body: Complaints and AMC visits logged against the original order, with full audit trail.

process.title: From Floor to Live System
process.lead: No two operations run the same way, so every build is scoped. The shape never changes: we start on your floor, build one module first, and hand it to real users.

steps.title: We walk the floor
steps.body: We visit. We follow one order end to end — enquiry, site measurement, job card, production, dispatch. We watch where the register is kept and where work actually stalls.
steps.deliverable: A written map of your current order flow, stage by stage.

steps.title: We agree the scope
steps.body: We list every module and rank them with you. The stage that hurts you daily gets built first. Scope, sequence and cost go into writing before work starts.
steps.deliverable: A signed scope: modules, order of build, and dates.

steps.title: First module live
steps.body: One module goes live with the people who use it — not a demo. Your staff enter real challans and job cards from day one. We sit beside them.
steps.deliverable: A working module on your floor, with your live data.

steps.title: Rollout across departments
steps.body: Departments come on in sequence — sales, design, production, stores, accounts, dispatch. Tally stays where it is; we feed it. Old registers run alongside until nobody reaches for them.
steps.deliverable: Full system in daily use, department by department, training done.

steps.title: We keep building
steps.body: You get a direct line to the person who built it, not a ticket queue. New stages, reports and series get added as the operation changes and grows.
steps.deliverable: A named contact for support, and a system that stays current.

process.note: Every engagement is scoped to the operation. The four-week deployment above is what happened at Pranav Doors & Windows. The sequence is always module-first.
process.whyLead: Most manufacturers weigh the same three options before calling us. Here is what each costs on the floor, and what we do instead.

comparisons.reality: It works until it doesn't. Files sit on one machine, versions drift, status lives in someone's head or a WhatsApp thread nobody can search.
comparisons.archflow: One record per order, from enquiry to gate pass. Every stage stamped with who did it and when. Nobody asks where the file is.

comparisons.reality: You bend your process to fit theirs. Long implementation, licence per user, half the modules stay unused because they were built for another industry.
comparisons.archflow: We build to your stages, your series numbering, your job card. Nothing you don't use. It fits because it was measured on your floor.

comparisons.reality: They build what the brief says. Nobody has stood at your dispatch gate, so the software misses how orders move. Handover ends the relationship.
comparisons.archflow: Manufacturing software is all we build. Ours has run daily at Pranav Doors & Windows since 2024. We stay after go-live, through your busy season.

proof.title: Running a real factory since 2024
proof.lead: Pranav Doors & Windows, a premium fenestration manufacturer in Chandigarh, has run its full order lifecycle on ArchFlow every day since 2024. Nothing is a pilot.
narrative: Before us, the operation ran on spreadsheets, registers and WhatsApp. Quotations were rebuilt by hand for every window. Site measurements arrived on paper. Production cut from printed lists already two revisions old. Dispatch loaded whatever the challan said. Nobody could answer where a single order actually stood.

We started where it hurt most: the BOM-aware quotation engine, live inside two weeks. Enquiry and site measurement followed. Then job cards through cutting, welding, hardware and QC. Dispatch, installation and snag closure came next, then service and accounts. Ten departments were live by week four.
changes: A 40-window quotation now leaves the same day it arrives, priced from the real bill of materials.
Site measurements are versioned against the order, so production never cuts to a dimension that has since changed.
Cutting lists and glass orders generate straight from the confirmed BOM — nobody retypes a size by hand.
Where an order stands — cutting, glass, dispatch, installation — is one search, not a round of phone calls.
Directors read live numbers — orders by stage, pending advances, dispatches due — instead of a sheet compiled at month-end.
proof.visitLine: The factory is a short drive from this table. Stand in the departments and watch real orders move.
proof.industriesLead: Fenestration is our home floor. The same spine — enquiry, BOM, production, dispatch, installation — carries these industries, each built to its own stages.

industries.name: Doors & Windows
industries.line: uPVC, aluminium and wooden lines. Quotation, series, cutting list, site installation.

industries.name: Metal Fabrication
industries.line: Job cards, client-supplied material, job-work challans, RA bills and site erection.

industries.name: PEB & Steel Buildings
industries.line: Tonne-based estimates, drawing approvals, truckload dispatch, erection progress and milestone billing.

industries.name: Modular Kitchens & Interiors
industries.line: Dealer orders, site measurement after tiling, cutting lists, installation, handover sign-off.

industries.name: Glass Processing
industries.line: Toughening batches, rack tracking, breakage and remakes, dispatch in drop order.

industries.name: Custom ERP
industries.line: One platform replacing scattered registers, per-seat licences and an eighteen-month rollout.

industries.name: CRM for Manufacturers
industries.line: Dealer networks, site visits, quotation revisions, follow-ups with owners and dates.

industries.name: Custom MIS
industries.line: Live dashboards, exception alerts, daily digests — nobody compiles anything at month-end.

proof.industriesNote: Your industry unlisted? The platform is custom-built every time, so the vertical matters less than whether your work has stages.
proof.closeTitle: Scoped per project. Nothing off the shelf.
proof.closeBody: There is no rate card. We map your workflow — usually on your own floor — then give you a scope and a firm number. You talk to the builders, not a sales layer.
